steps.title: Read the graphed line
steps.content: The line shown is $y=f(x)+11$. Two clear grid points on that line are $(-4,9)$ and $(0,1)$. The slope is $$\frac{1-9}{0-(-4)}=\frac{-8}{4}=-2,$$ and the $y$-intercept is $1$, so the shown line is $y=-2x+1$.

steps.title: Match to the form with positive $c,d$
steps.content: Rewrite as $f(x)=-10-2x=-d-cx$ with $d=10$ and $c=2$, both positive. That matches choice A.
